name: Paladin
description: A paladin is a holy warrior, imbued with divine magic and bound by a sacred oath to pursue justice and righteousness. They wield a massive two-handed sword and wear full plate armor, serving as a beacon of hope and a bulwark against evil. Paladins are trained from a young age in both martial combat and divine prayer, and their conviction grants them the ability to channel divine energy through their weapon and hands alike.
tactics: The paladin leads from the front, engaging the most dangerous enemy or any dark or undead creature present. They open with a Greatsword Strike and follow up with a Smiting Strike to deal devastating combined damage. Pommel Strike is used to disable spellcasters or interrupt dangerous enemies. Lay on Hands is reserved for a critically wounded ally. The paladin will not retreat while allies are in danger.

actions.name: Greatsword Strike
actions.description: The paladin makes an attack with their two-handed sword, dealing physical damage.

actions.name: Smiting Strike
actions.description: The paladin channels divine energy through their greatsword, dealing physical and divine damage. Standard and lower dark and undead creatures that take any divine damage from this attack must make a resilience check of 6 or higher or become stunned until the end of their next turn. Champion and elite undead and dark creatures become dazed instead.

actions.name: Lay on Hands
actions.description: The paladin touches a humanoid and channels divine healing, healing for 20 health and curing any physical conditions that are not due to permanent effects.

traits.name: Divine Warrior
traits.description: The paladin gains a bonus die on attack checks and resilience checks against dark and undead creatures. This bonus also applies to creatures who have channeled a spell from the dark school of magic within the last turn.

traits.name: Aura of Courage
traits.description: The paladin is immune to the afraid condition. Allies within a very short (5 unit) radius gain a bonus die on checks to resist or recover from the afraid condition.
